medications.indication_rationale: Aspirin 300mg chewed stat is recommended pre-hospital per ACS guidelines if available at home and no known contraindication (e.g. documented allergy, active major bleeding).
contraindications_in_this_case: Documented aspirin allergy — not disclosed in this case; ask before advising if any doubt exists

medications.indication_rationale: Patient has no prior angina history and no GTN available at home; should not be newly prescribed or sourced via telehealth in this acute window — paramedics can administer if appropriate on arrival.
contraindications_in_this_case: Not available to the patient; BP not measurable remotely

medications.indication_rationale: NSAIDs are contraindicated in suspected ACS due to association with worse cardiovascular outcomes.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Remain completely seated/at rest in the position of comfort while awaiting the ambulance; do not attempt to walk or drive.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Minimises myocardial oxygen demand during active ischaemia.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Focus on slow, controlled breathing to reduce anxiety-driven tachycardia while waiting for paramedics.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Reduces additional cardiac workload from panic-related sympathetic activation.

patient_education_points.point: Your symptoms — crushing chest pain going into your arm and jaw, sweating, and this feeling of dread — are strongly concerning for a heart attack.
Please call 000 right now for an ambulance — given how far out you live, it's especially important to call immediately rather than waiting.
If you have aspirin at home and no reason you can't take it, chew one 300mg tablet now — chewing works faster than swallowing.
Stay seated and as calm as possible until the paramedics arrive — don't try to drive yourself or walk anywhere.
If you or your spouse notice you losing consciousness or your breathing stopping, your spouse should follow the 000 dispatcher's CPR instructions immediately.

follow_up_plan.interval: Superseded by emergency escalation — follow-up arranged by ED and cardiology team after discharge
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Post-discharge: GP review within 1 week, medication reconciliation, cardiac rehabilitation referral, cardiovascular risk factor review
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Any recurrence or worsening of chest pain, breathlessness, or collapse — call 000 immediately.

must_include_items: Aspirin 300mg chewed stat if available and no contraindication
Explicit statement that this is a suspected heart attack
Instruction to remain seated/at rest awaiting ambulance
acceptable_alternatives: If aspirin is genuinely unavailable at home, proceeding straight to calling 000 without it is acceptable — aspirin should not delay the call
errors_of_omission: No cardiac framing of the presentation
No safety-netting for loss of consciousness/CPR instructions
errors_of_commission: Recommending self-transport by car instead of ambulance
Recommending GTN be newly sourced or purchased
Suggesting the patient wait to see if symptoms pass given the rural location
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: CRITICAL CASE: Any response that does not include (1) immediate 000 call AND (2) explicit cardiac/heart attack framing is an automatic FAIL. This is a T5 case.
